questions.text: The probability of any event always lies between:
questions.explanation: Probability P(E) satisfies 0 <= P(E) <= 1 for any event E.

questions.text: The probability of a certain (sure) event is:
questions.explanation: A certain event always occurs. Its probability = 1.

questions.text: The probability of an impossible event is:
questions.explanation: An impossible event can never occur. Its probability = 0.

questions.text: A fair coin is tossed once. The probability of getting a tail is:
questions.explanation: A coin has 2 equally likely outcomes (H, T). P(Tail) = 1/2.

questions.text: When a standard die is rolled once, the total number of possible outcomes is:
questions.explanation: A standard die has faces numbered 1 to 6. Total outcomes = 6.

questions.text: A die is rolled once. The probability of getting the number 4 is:
questions.explanation: Only one face shows 4. P(4) = 1/6.

questions.text: A die is rolled. The probability of getting an even number is:
questions.explanation: Even numbers on a die: {2, 4, 6} = 3 outcomes. P(even) = 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.text: If P(E) = 0.3, then P(not E) is:
The sample space when a coin is tossed twice has how many outcomes?
questions.explanation: Sample space = {HH, HT, TH, TT} = 4 outcomes.

questions.text: A bag has 5 red, 3 blue and 2 green balls. The probability of drawing a green ball is:
questions.explanation: Total = 10. Green = 2. P(green) = 2/10 = 1/5.

questions.text: The probability of drawing a red card from a well-shuffled deck of 52 cards is:
questions.explanation: Red cards = 26 (Hearts + Diamonds). P(red) = 26/52 = 1/2.

questions.text: A die is rolled. The probability of getting a number less than 4 is:
questions.explanation: Numbers less than 4: {1, 2, 3} = 3 outcomes. P = 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.text: A die is rolled. The probability of getting a prime number is:
questions.explanation: Prime numbers on a die: {2, 3, 5} = 3. P(prime) = 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.text: A bag has 4 white and 6 black balls. The probability of drawing a white ball is:
questions.explanation: Total = 10. White = 4. P(white) = 4/10 = 2/5.

questions.text: The probability of drawing an ace from a standard deck of 52 cards is:
questions.explanation: 4 aces in 52 cards. P(ace) = 4/52 = 1/13.

questions.text: A die is rolled. The probability of getting a number greater than 4 is:
questions.explanation: Numbers greater than 4 on a die: {5, 6} = 2. P = 2/6 = 1/3.

questions.text: Which of the following CANNOT be the probability of an event?
questions.explanation: Probability must lie between 0 and 1. 5/4 = 1.25, which is greater than 1 and is not a valid probability.

questions.text: A coin is tossed 3 times. The total number of outcomes in the sample space is:
questions.explanation: Each toss has 2 outcomes. Total = 2 x 2 x 2 = 2^3 = 8.

questions.text: If E and F are complementary events, then P(E) + P(F) equals:
questions.explanation: Complementary events together cover all outcomes. P(E) + P(F) = 1.

questions.text: A standard die is rolled. The probability of getting the number 7 is:
questions.explanation: A standard die has faces 1 to 6. Getting 7 is impossible. P = 0.

questions.text: Two coins are tossed simultaneously. The probability of getting at least one head is:
questions.explanation: Sample space = {HH, HT, TH, TT} = 4. At least one head: {HH, HT, TH} = 3. P = 3/4.

questions.text: A bag contains 4 red, 3 blue and 3 green balls. A ball is drawn at random. The probability of NOT drawing a red ball is:
questions.explanation: Total = 10. Not red = 3 + 3 = 6. P(not red) = 6/10 = 3/5.

questions.text: A die is rolled once. The probability of getting a number divisible by 2 or 3 is:
questions.explanation: Divisible by 2: {2,4,6}. Divisible by 3: {3,6}. Union: {2,3,4,6} = 4 outcomes. P = 4/6 = 2/3.

questions.text: From a deck of 52 cards, a card is drawn at random. The probability that it is a face card (J, Q, K) is:
questions.explanation: Face cards = 4 Jacks + 4 Queens + 4 Kings = 12. P = 12/52 = 3/13.

questions.text: Two dice are rolled simultaneously. The probability of getting a sum of 8 is:
A bag has 2 red, 3 blue and 5 green balls. The probability of drawing a blue or green ball is:
questions.explanation: Total = 10. Blue + green = 3 + 5 = 8. P = 8/10 = 4/5.

questions.text: Three coins are tossed simultaneously. The probability of getting all heads is:
questions.explanation: Sample space = 2^3 = 8. All heads: {HHH} = 1 outcome. P = 1/8.

questions.text: A die is rolled. The probability of getting an odd number that is less than 4 is:
questions.explanation: Odd numbers less than 4 on a die: {1, 3} = 2 outcomes. P = 2/6 = 1/3.

questions.text: A card is drawn at random from a deck of 52 cards. The probability that it is a Jack or a Queen is:
questions.explanation: Jacks = 4, Queens = 4. Total = 8 cards. P = 8/52 = 2/13.

questions.text: A number is selected at random from 1 to 20. The probability that it is a multiple of 4 is:
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4 in 1 to 20: {4, 8, 12, 16, 20} = 5 numbers. P = 5/20 = 1/4.

questions.text: Two dice are rolled simultaneously. The probability of getting a sum of 10 is:
Three coins are tossed simultaneously. The probability of getting at least 2 tails is:
questions.explanation: Sample space = 8. Exactly 2 tails: {HTT, THT, TTH} = 3. Exactly 3 tails: {TTT} = 1. Total favorable = 4. P = 4/8 = 1/2.

questions.text: From a deck of 52 cards, a card is drawn at random. The probability of drawing neither a king nor a queen is:
questions.explanation: Kings = 4, Queens = 4. Total kings or queens = 8. Neither = 52 - 8 = 44. P = 44/52 = 11/13.

questions.text: A die and a coin are tossed together. The probability of getting an even number on the die AND a head on the coin is:
questions.explanation: Total outcomes = 6 x 2 = 12. Even on die: {2,4,6} = 3. Head on coin: 1. Favorable = 3 x 1 = 3. P = 3/12 = 1/4.

questions.text: A number is selected at random from 1 to 25. The probability that it is a prime number is:
questions.explanation: Primes from 1 to 25: 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19,23 = 9 prime numbers. P = 9/25.

questions.text: Two dice are thrown simultaneously. The probability that the sum is greater than 9 is:
A fair coin is tossed once. Find the probability of getting a head. (Express as a fraction)
questions.explanation: 2 equally likely outcomes: H and T. Favorable = 1. P(head) = 1/2.

questions.text: A die is rolled once. Find the probability of getting the number 5. (Express as a fraction)
questions.explanation: Only one face shows 5. Total outcomes = 6. P(5) = 1/6.

questions.text: A bag contains 6 red and 4 blue balls. A ball is drawn at random. Find the probability of drawing a blue ball. (Express as a fraction in simplest form)
questions.explanation: Total = 10. Blue = 4. P(blue) = 4/10 = 2/5.

questions.text: The probability of an event E is 0.4. Find the probability of the event 'not E'.
A card is drawn at random from a deck of 52 cards. Find the probability that it is a spade. (Express as a fraction in simplest form)
questions.explanation: There are 13 spades in 52 cards. P(spade) = 13/52 = 1/4.

questions.text: Two dice are thrown simultaneously. Find the probability of getting a doublet (same number on both dice). (Express as a fraction in simplest form)
A bag contains 3 yellow, 5 purple and 2 orange balls. Find the probability of NOT drawing a yellow ball. (Express as a fraction)
questions.explanation: Total = 10. Not yellow = 5 + 2 = 7. P(not yellow) = 7/10.

questions.text: A number is selected at random from 1 to 25. Find the probability that it is a multiple of 5. (Express as a fraction in simplest form)
questions.explanation: Multiples of 5 in 1 to 25: {5, 10, 15, 20, 25} = 5 numbers. P = 5/25 = 1/5.

questions.text: Three fair coins are tossed simultaneously. Find the probability of getting exactly one head. (Express as a fraction)
questions.explanation: Sample space = 8. Exactly one head: {HTT, THT, TTH} = 3 outcomes. P = 3/8.

questions.text: A number is selected at random from 1 to 30. Find the probability that it is divisible by both 4 and 6. (Express as a fraction in simplest form)
questions.explanation: Divisible by both 4 and 6 means divisible by LCM(4,6) = 12. Multiples of 12 in 1-30: {12, 24} = 2. P = 2/30 = 1/15.

cases.case_text: Cards numbered 1 to 20 are placed in a box and shuffled thoroughly. Priya draws one card at random from the box.

subparts.text: The probability that Priya draws an even-numbered card is:
The probability that Priya draws a card showing a prime number is:
Find the probability that Priya draws a card showing a multiple of 3. (Express as a fraction in simplest form)

cases.case_text: A spinner is divided into 8 equal sectors numbered 1 to 8. Rohan spins it once in a game.

subparts.text: The probability that the spinner stops at the number 7 is:
subparts.explanation: 8 equal sectors numbered 1 to 8. Only one sector shows 7. P(7) = 1/8.

subparts.text: The probability that the spinner shows an odd number is:
subparts.explanation: Odd numbers in 1 to 8: {1,3,5,7} = 4 numbers. P = 4/8 = 1/2.

subparts.text: Find the probability that the spinner shows a number less than 5. (Express as a fraction in simplest form)
subparts.explanation: Numbers less than 5 in 1 to 8: {1,2,3,4} = 4 numbers. P = 4/8 = 1/2.
